This directly supports the need for either removing the content-encoding header or re-encoding the modified body to align with the header. Additional excerpts confirm that route.fulfill can override or pass through response details, including headers, and that route.fetch() provides a decompressed body, which informs how to prepare a correctly fulfilled response. Supporting details about the capability to monitor and modify HTTP(S) traffic via Playwright route APIs and how to use glob/regex predicates for matching further contextualize the mechanism, though they are ancillary to the core issue of compressed body handling. The combination of documented bugs and API behavior informs the correct handling approach and clarifies why the content-encoding header must be reconciled with the actual (possibly recompressed) payload.
